results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE GOAL OF THE PROPOSED RESEARCH IS TO IMPROVE THE CHEMICAL MECHANISM REPRESENTATION OF SECONDARY ORGANIC AEROSOL (SOA) FORMATION FROM OXYGENATED VOLATILE ORGANIC COMPOUNDS (VOCS) IN BIOMASS BURNING AND VOLATILE CHEMICAL PRODUCTS (VCP) SOURCES. THIS PROPOSAL HYPOTHESIZES THAT THE INCLUSION OF OXYGENATED VOCS, PARTICULARLY PHENOLS, FURANS, GLYCOLS, AND GLYCOL ETHERS, IN CHEMICAL MECHANISMS WILL IMPROVE PREDICTIONS OF SOA FORMATION AND PROPERTIES BY AIR QUALITY MODELS (AQMS).ACTIVITIES:THIS RESEARCH WILL LEAD TO IMPROVED CHEMICAL MECHANISMS TO REPRESENT OXYGENATED VOLATILE ORGANIC COMPOUNDS IN AIR QUALITY MODELING. THE PRIMARY TOOL WE WILL USE IN THIS WORK IS THE SOM-TOMAS (STATISTICAL OXIDATION MODEL- TWO MOMENT AEROSOL SECTIONAL) MODEL THAT SIMULATES THE MULTIGENERATIONAL CHEMISTRY, THERMODYNAMIC PROPERTIES, AND MICROPHYSICS OF SECONDARY ORGANIC AEROSOLS. THE RECIPIENT WILL DEVELOP AN IMPROVED REPRESENTATION OF SECONDARY ORGANIC AEROSOL FORMATION FROM OXYGENATED VOLATILE ORGANIC COMPOUNDS IN BIOMASS BURNING AND VOLATILE CHEMICAL PRODUCT SOURCES, WHICH WILL LEAD TO IMPROVED PERFORMANCE OF AIR QUALITY MODELS, THEREBY PRIORITIZING ROBUST SCIENCE.SUBRECIPIENT:OUTCOMES:THE OUTCOMES OF THIS RESEARCH WILL IMPROVE THE ABILITY OF CHEMICAL MECHANISMS TO MORE ACCURATELY REPRESENT HOW SECONDARY ORGANIC AEROSOLS CONTRIBUTE TO FINE PARTICLE POLLUTION FROM BIOMASS BURNING AND VOLATILE CHEMICAL PRODUCTS. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROGRAM INCLUDE THE SCIENTIFIC COMMUNITY; STATE, LOCAL, AND TRIBAL AIR QUALITY AGENCIES; AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC.
